Subject: RE: ISR Recruitment Outsourcing
Tracy,
Peak is way to expensive. They charge $10k to start search, $10k for initial interviews (3) and
then once placed 4 x $8 for the first year – basically the whole salary for an ISR. I almost fell
out.
I am searching more firms including those you mentioned. Stay tuned. Dina and I have
spoken and have a plan. Donna
Donna Millard – Vice President of People & Culture

www.tevet.com
From: Tracy Solomon <tracy.solomon@tevet.com>
Sent: Wednesday, August 6, 2025 5:21 PM
To: Donna Millard <donna.millard@Tevet.com>; Dina Brouse <dina.brouse@Tevet.com>
Cc: William Bracken <william.bracken@tevet.com>
Subject: RE: ISR Recruitment Outsourcing
Ladies,
I am excited to hear more about Peak! Look forward to speaking with them when
convenient.
I am looking for collaboration and alignment of what criteria we are going to use in down
selecting firms to a final choice. For example, can we all agree that “Process Rigor” is an
important criterion? If so, then Peak would be at the top, which is what I heard from both of
you about them. With a full house this week I was not able to get this communicate to as
concise a place as I had wanted. This is lot, but I want to ensure that everyone understands
where my head is, that we collaborate, and we achieve alignment. My hope is that this is a
one and done.
EXAMPLES:
These are examples and not what I would call final criteria. For example, I don’t know that
Defense Contractor Experience is important in this search vs my top priority which is
selecting someone that can perform in recruiting, “Passive Headhunting”, from the list of
target companies that we have provided. i.e. T&M Distribution. Can either of you tell me
how Peak performed in that category from your discussion?
At the end of the day the firm selected has to produce results in vetting and producing
candidates that we consider as “Best Athlete”. If the hiring manger is not having issue with
selecting one from candidates selected then the recruiter is not meeting expectations.
Sample Criteria Weight Explanation
T&M OEM/Distributor Prior placements from or knowledge of Keysight, NI,

Page 4

Expertise R&S, Newark, etc.
Defense Contractor Familiarity with selling to or servicing Raytheon,
25%

Passive Candidate

professional sales DNA

onboarding support

(30%)

Sales Talent

Inc.

Staff

⃣ Recruiting
Direct
5 ⚠️ 5/10 ⚠️ 5/10 ✅ 8/10 ✅ 7/10 ✅ 8/10 66
⃣ Recruiters Inc.
My Specific Thoughts:
As the Owner and CEO, my top three priorities when selecting a recruiter revolve around
protecting our time, elevating our brand, and ensuring every hire moves the company
forward. Here’s how that breaks down into five mission-critical “cares abouts” from my
chair:
1. Domain Expertise & Role Fluency
Do they understand what you actually sell—and who we are selling to?
Know-how in test & measurement, distribution, or technical sales
Ability to qualify candidates for DoD channel nuance, OEM partnerships, and
regulated industries
Recognizes a BS artist vs. someone who can actually deliver value to a Lockheed or
Raytheon

Page 5
2. Proven Passive Talent Access
Can they pull top performers from your competitors, not just post a job and pray?
Uses headhunting, not job boards
Brings you people who are killing it somewhere else, not ones who just got fired
Has access to hidden, high-performing candidates in your space
3. Trust, Discretion & Cultural Fit
Will they represent your company the way you would?
Aligns with TEVET’s integrity, mission-driven tone, and high standards
You can trust them to challenge you when you're off-mark on role scope or
compensation
Doesn’t make you babysit the search process or clean up after them
4. Track Record in Similar Hires
Have they done this exact job, for someone like you, and done it well?
Ask: “Can you show me 2–3 hires you’ve made in the last year in test/measurement, or
adjacent to A&D?”
Look for: Titles like Inside Sales Manager, Director of Marketing, Technical AE, etc.
Prefer referrals or case studies over polished decks
5. Speed, Follow-Through, and Execution Rigor
Do they move fast, stay on top of the process, and get it done—without drama?
Average time-to-present (5–10 business days is elite)
Responsiveness and urgency match your tempo
Don’t go dark. Don’t make you chase. Don’t cut corners.
Includes candidate briefings, call notes, offer-stage insights, and counteroffer prep
My opinion on why Passive Headhunting Matters to TEVET.
For roles like:
Inside Sales with test & measurement knowledge
Account Managers from key competitors
We have rarely going to find them applying to your open position. They’re already:
Hitting quota
Loyal to their employer
Comfortable where they are
We need to steal their attention, not wait for it.
What I do NOT Want to Prioritize:

Page 6
These criteria are more in line with my thoughts than examples I showed above:
Score Weighted
Criteria Weight Notes
(1-10) Score

etc.?

10%

This is sample checklist that I will use when I speak to Peak or any others:

Page 7
Regards,
Tracy Solomon – CEO

Subject: Re: ISR Recruitment Outsourcing
Donna,
My second and third were Direct and Egret Consulting. Did you receive anything back from
them?
Dina, as we look to down select do you have criteria that you want us to focus in on? My
initial criteria was for firms that focus on our industry and currently recruit heavily from the
firms that we have identified as targets.
All, as a side note some of these firms do more than just inside sales and can recruit for
Account Managers, VP of Sales, etc.
Regards,
Tracy Solomon – CEO

I have reviewed all three outsourcing firms for the ISR recruitment potential. Until you meet

Transcript

August 26, 2025, 6:00PM

Donna Millard started transcription

Carly Van Gogh 0:03

OK. Are is the company located in Vancouver?

Yeah.

So yeah.

So our company technically is Canadian, 90% of our business is US based and 90% of our workforce.
We all work remotely. Works on the East Coast of Canada or the US.
So the majority of our business is done in America.
I'm one of two individuals in the whole company living on the West Coast.

Donna Millard 0:41
Well, thank you for making time for us. So let me explain what what we what's what a little bit about tevet.
So we are in the aerospace defense arena.

Yep.

Raising Yep.
Understood.

Their car to account.
We already have key accounts and then we assign an account manager to manage one of those customers and maybe more. Some of them have more than one, you know customer and then they have a small team of the inside sales reps that support that.
So that's the dynamic between these two positions.

Carly Van Gogh 1:26
Understood.

Donna Millard 1:28
Why are we asking for assistance?
We have found that, for instance, the whole psychometric testing you're doing.
We do API certainly when somebody if we screen a resume.
We send it to the manager. Manners bring him in for an interview.
We immediately shoot them a predictive index, which is a similar product.

Carly Van Gogh 1:46
Yep.

Donna Millard 1:47
However, you go beyond that because you do behavioral interviewing and you do all these other things.
So I kind of understand the whole P-95 part of what you offer.
We have not had successful placements.
We don't know if it's a failure on our team to really screen the candidates, but for instance, we hired an account manager who came from a similar industry.
What, like five months ago or four months ago, Bambi and she only stayed for, like, 60 days.

Mm-hmm.

Donna Millard 2:15
She said she prefers outside sales.
She was sold a different job than she thought it was going to be coming in.
Our inside sales team has a brand new leader, so the current people in on that team.

Carly Van Gogh 2:24
OK.

Donna Millard 2:29

So what the teams did is oh, you're good at that.
Do it.
You're good at that.
Do it.
But they really were not bucketed, so this is going to be the begin sale group. This is going to be mid sale.

Carly Van Gogh 2:38
Yeah.

Donna Millard 2:44
This is going to be final sale, so quote customer relations. Yeah, right.

Carly Van Gogh 2:48
Qualify. Yep.

Donna Millard 2:50
So there was none of that.
And So what happened is some of them became very comfortable doing part of the job and not all of it.

Carly Van Gogh 2:52
Hmm.

Donna Millard 2:56
O the new sales leader came in and she reassessed the situation.
She's trying to work with the existing members, but they're down to people and they have a couple pregnancies that trying to prepare for.
So what?
Our CEO told us.
Or me. He wants to build a long term relationship with one recruitment partner for all of our sales needs today and tomorrow.
As of today, we have, like I said, the two account manager positions are open.

Carly Van Gogh 3:22
Yep.

Donna Millard 3:26
But that we signed a contract with another sort of local recruiter who already has some people in the pipeline.

Carly Van Gogh 3:33
OK.

Donna Millard 3:34
So the what what?
The ASK is right now to begin with. Peak would be to fill the two ISR jobs inside sales representative jobs.
But Tevit is willing if we know you have candidates for both, we're willing to a look at them.
And B, you know, make a commitment if we select you.
We would make you our exclusive recruitment source for all of sales.

Amazing. Thank you so much for that, Donna.
That's an excellent overview. I appreciate it.

Donna Millard 4:03
Yeah.
No, thank you for that.

No, I really let me break that down into components.

We are not interested in being a vendor and doing 1 placement or doing a single experience and then you know, not having our relationship continue to be completely transparent.

Donna Millard 4:27
Right.

Carly Van Gogh 4:28
Again, we are the best.
At sales recruiting overall in North America as a whole, I am completely confident so to this measure, we want to ensure that our clients know that they can rely on us for their evolution, for their growth. You know for what their needs are today and for their up.
Needs as well. So we think the same number one, number two, in terms of the contract that we've talked about already, I completely understand that the focal point right now is the inside sales reps and that the account managers are also needed.
But right now this is being managed by another firm.
No problem.

Donna Millard 5:02
Right.

Carly Van Gogh 5:03
You still have the opportunity to sign off with us for these first four.
Thus maximizing the volume discount we can offer and having the ability to change up those positions if and when these account managers are fulfilled by this firm and you'd like to change that into two more Isrs or two more account managers down the road, the flexibility there ex.
For you and I think.

Donna Millard 5:26
OK.

That is likely the best way to move ahead.
If we are selected because again we are aligned in wanting to supply all of your sales staffing needs. So whether or not we utilize this particular and inaugural contract the way that we split it right now or not, it's still in your interest in terms of cost and.

OK. That is. That's wonderful, Carly.
Thank you.
So I know you do executive search as well.
We are going to be looking for a vice President of sales, so ideally you know if peak has an arsenal available to recruit and I noticed that you did on your website you know then that would likely perhaps be another position we would outsource to you as well.

Yes.
OK.
Yep.

So our CEO is very open minded.

Carly Van Gogh 6:16
Awesome.

He wants quality and so.

Thank you.

Donna Millard 6:27
You really do vet the candidates.
I did have a few questions about the process though.

Yes, please.

So obviously we would be assigned a team.
Let's just call the peak team for recruitment.
So initially it's a cultural assessment of the company, making sure that you guys lock down specifically what it is we're looking for in these candidates.
The must haves and that kind of thing.
Then you move into the phase of actually head hunting, which is passive recruitment going against competitors looking in the market and that kind of thing.

Correct. And there is one other step that I just want to mention very quickly in that beginning part of the process what you described, Donna, what you did beautifully.
Thank you. Is kick off.
So this is where you're meeting with your team and we are, you know, taking a high level approach to understanding exactly as you suggested, the ideal vision for these candidates across these various positions.
You as a whole, the company, you know, culture, core values.
What the organization offers in terms of growth trajectory for these individuals?
As well, because keep in mind it's equally our responsibility to sell these amazing opportunities to top talent as it is for us to find top talent for you.

Right.

So the more we understand about why it's great to work with and for you, the better #1 after that meeting 24 hours later, we have our second call and this is the calibration exercise. In this call, the team will reconvene with you and actually present a.
Handful of sample profiles that we already have in our network.
That we think align with the vision that you outlined for us and the day before regarding your Unicorn.
The reason this is really important is because a it gives us the opportunity to ensure not just ensure that we know exactly the type of profile that you're after and that you expect. And Yep, gives us the opportunity, if you do say yes, this is great but or.

That's outstanding.

No, no, no. Hold on.
There's something missing that we bridge that gap before we start.
Because we don't want to be guessing.

OK.

There's no room for that.
There's no margin for that type of error.
So that second call is equally as valuable and important.

Donna Millard 8:38
OK.
Fair enough.

Carly Van Gogh 8:39
Yep.

So let me just I had a few questions.
Let me just make sure I knock some of those out. So you have a 95% success rate.

Please.

Great question. There is a different number.

120 yeah, the 120 days.

You know what, Donna?
We have a huge long list of clients in this space and actually my team is working on getting you those specifics now. But what I will say is that the aerospace industry and those that sell into this space are a huge vertical for us. And so again there.

Donna Millard 9:45
OK, OK.

Carly Van Gogh 9:55
Already a team in mind that would be dedicated and assigned to you both and to tevit for exactly this reason.

OK.

Carly Van Gogh 10:00
That information is coming.
I just don't want to misquote or give you details that aren't.
Proper so I will e-mail that information to you as soon as possible, but it's tremendous for us.

No, no worries. Thank you.
I think I might have told you. We do have one more meeting tomorrow and then Bambi and I were tasked with doing a side by side comparison. And honestly, like I said, I mean, I haven't heard their pitch yet, but I mean, just based on what you offer.

Yep.

MEMORANDUM
Date: March 23, 2026
From: Donna Millard, VP of HR, People & Culture
Subject: Summary of Telephonic Meeting with Peak Sales Recruitment – March 23 at 2:00 PM EST
Attendees:
• Connor Fletcher, Account Manager, Peak Sales Recruitment
• Sam Murka, Regional Manager, Peak Sales Recruitment
• Tracy Solomon, CEO, TEVET
• Sarah Zamudio, Contracts Manager, TEVET
• Emilee Askin, Manager, Recruitment & Onboarding, TEVET
• Donna Millard, VP, HR, TEVET
Meeting Summary
A telephonic meeting was held with Peak Sales Recruitment to address ongoing concerns regarding
their performance and results over the past 6–7 months in support of TEVET’s Sales recruitment
efforts.
Key Discussion Points
1. CEO’s Concerns on Performance
• The CEO expressed clear dissatisfaction with Peak Sales’ efforts since their selection from a
group of highly recommended firms.
• At the initial kick-off call, TEVET provided a specific list of target companies relevant to our
sector for sourcing Account Manager (AM) and Inside Sales Representative (ISR) candidates.
Peak Sales’ prior recruiter did not utilize that list.
o
As a result, candidates sourced were misaligned, one hire ultimately left after a short
o
period, and provided feedback indicating they were pushed to take the position even
with her doubts about the job. She resigned in 4 weeks.
• Examples highlighted:
One candidate stated they would have responded earlier had they known the
o
opportunity was with TEVET.
Another ISR hire shared during an exit interview that they were coached to “give it a
o
try” despite concerns about role fit.

Page 2
2. Peak Sales’ Explanation of Their Process
• Peak explained their use of a passive hiring strategy, initially withholding the company name
to generate candidate interest.
• The CEO reiterated that openly leading with TEVET’s name would attract stronger candidates
from targeted industry competitors such as Test Equity and Newark and he wanted them to
do this – even it means them changing their process for us.
3. Discussion on Contractual Obligations
• The CEO stated he is seriously considering requesting a refund ($70k already spent) due to
lost time, resources, and delays in critical hiring. Stating we are now back where we were
when this started.
• Mr. Murka responded that refunds are not included in their contract, but replacements are
fully supported and they would work hard to get them filled.
4. Current Status of Active Searches
• Connor noted he is currently sourcing heavily from the target companies provided and
acknowledged that, aside from two VP Sales candidates, the search remains ongoing.
• He identified one promising AM candidate, Ryan Hofmockel, whom Jerri expressed strong
interest in.
Ryan meets with TEVET’s VP of Liberty Sales this week.
o
• For the ISR role, the two internal employee referrals TEVET provided are progressing and
being vetted, and additional candidate has been sourced.
5. TEVET’s Next Steps and Expectations
• The CEO stated Peak needs to have an internal discussion about whether they can truly meet
TEVET’s needs. Termination of the relationship is under consideration.
• TEVET communicated that Emilee is now fully onboard and will use the same sourcing tools
as Peak Sales, which will provide insight into whether TEVET is able to uncover talent Peak is
not presenting.
Conclusion of Meeting
Peak Sales reaffirmed their desire to continue supporting TEVET. Connor stated he is now fully
aligned on expectations and looks forward to collaborating closely with Emilee to accelerate
progress.

From: Jerri Gore

Subject: RE: Peak Sales Call 3.31.26 Summary of Call.
Date: Friday, March 27, 2026 8:46:21 AM
Hi all,
Tracy has detailed our direction and actions very well. My additional comments inserted
below-
Jerri Gore
Director of Sales & Strategic Partnerships
__________________________________
TEVET - Your VETERAN Source!
SDVOSB & HUBZone Small Business
O: 678.905.1300 ext. 141
C: 423.823.4843
jerri.gore@tevet.com
www.tevet.com
From: Tracy Solomon <tracy.solomon@tevet.com>
Sent: Thursday, March 26, 2026 6:30 PM
To: Donna Millard <donna.millard@Tevet.com>; Emilee Askin <emilee.askin@Tevet.com>; Jerri Gore
<jerri.gore@tevet.com>; Sarah Zamudio <sarah.zamudio@Tevet.com>
Cc: Corky Clodfelter <corkyc@appliedhr.com>; Candace Deuster <candace.deuster@Tevet.com>;
John Vandewater <john.vandewater@tevet.com>
Subject: FW: Peak Sales Call 3.31.26 Summary of Call.
All,
Donna prepared the attached notes as her recollection of this week’s call with Peak.
Does this cover all aspects from everyone else recollection? I am adding Jerri as she has
been highly engaged with Peek over this past year as well and will likely add flavor.
I want to make sure that we hit on everything as Connor has asked that I or we put our
issues in an email back to him for escalation. As much as I would like to fire them, they
owe us positions that we have paid for and we need this information both to update
Connor and his management as to how things have went down, but we also must
educate any new recruiters that we work with.
I see this as a good outline of the call but it misses key points that I made with Connor
and his management and that we need to be very specific about both with them and any

Page 2
new recruiting firm. I have added points a they come to mind.
1. TEVET HR was directed to lead a sourcing effort last year to partner with a
recruiting firm that specializes in sourcing sales professional’s. Our previous
recruiting firm, Matt Weitz, was good at sourcing industry AM’s but would not
recruit for ISR’s.
2. The award was based on which firm had the ability to SPECIFICALLY deliver
candidates from the target list of companies. Peek SOLD us at the time that they
were the ones who could accomplish this.
3. TEVET was very specific from the beginning as to what success looked like. We
spent much time with PEAK early on providing specific resumes from people we
sourced from industry. Chrissy is our target candidate for AM who came from Test
Equity. Find us more… Kristen is the target ISR who came from Newark. Find us
more… We set up meetings between Peak and these employees, provided
resumes along with JD’s and Predictive Index information.
We also spoke in great detail about what attributes and characteristics a
successful candidate would embody; sense of urgency, proactive, outbound,
problem solving. We also spoke about market (T&M) and customer knowledge
(A/D) being a key marker in the candidates. For the AM role, we emphasized to an
even greater focus on the DOE specifically.
4. We notified them that is traditionally took us six months to train up ISR’s or AM’s.
We had tried recruiting from industry manufacture’s and elsewhere and that had
repeatedly failed. This was to be a targeted sourcing effort from the industry
companies provided to them in a ranked fashion.
There was an very extensive list of target companies provided and ranked in order
of value. For ISR role, we didn’t see any from this list.
5. It is folly to think that we can recruit from anyone outside of the target companies
and think that they would be able to spin up well enough for us to make a decision
in 90 days.
6. It is likely that TEVET will terminate the remaining two ISR’s currently employed.
7. The CEO has had to lean in repeatedly with Peak to dive into what’s actually going
over the months of them not brining the candidates forward.
8. TEVET caught peak in several lies or misdirects

from the list provided. I argue that this was make clear up front and they were
selected solely on their assuring us of their ability to be successful with these
companies.

candidate’s from the targeted companies. I directed him start leading with TEVET

Page 3
immediately once I found out he was not.
11. TEVET had an ISR resign three weeks ago. Peak indicated on the call they were not
aware of it or were not actively searching as TEVET had not opened up another req
with them.
12. TEVET told them specifically to tell applicants from industry who they were
recruiting for. We explained our position and reputation in the market. We learned
later that they were NOT sharing this information, or using it as a recruiting tool
I don’t have time tonight to keep going on this. I would ask that HR pull all of these
thoughts together in a succinct but comprehensive manner so that we can fully present
the facts back to Connor in written form which he has asked that I do.

months for internal documentation. Will do!
Donna, you likely have more direct communication with Peak that needs to added to the
mix. I don’t recall how you ran the solicitation or down selected to Peak.
Compile what you can. We can have a meeting to discuss, cover everything while we
transcribe our thoughts and drop into GPT to wrap it up.
I’ve asked Donna and John to give me the internal numbers (estimates) of what this has
cost TEVET. Time invested, salaries paid, etc.
I repeated in this week’s meeting to Peak that what we have experienced / endured has
been egregious. I do not take lightly the lives affected and families impacted both on our
side due to our inability to answer the call over the past year as well as the candidates
and their families who are negatively impacted.
And then there is the legal exposure that we are being exposed to in this process.

updated before I send to Tracy:
Connor,
Following up on our prior conversation, I wanted to put TEVET’s concerns in writing so you
can share them internally and help us work toward a fair resolution.
While we are concerned with both of the contracts we’ve signed with Peak Sales, our
primary concern is with the ISR portion of the engagement.
TEVET selected Peak Sales in large part because Peak Sales represented that it could

Page 2
recruit from our targeted list of companies and had access to candidates within our
industry, including aerospace and defense, T&M, and distribution. That capability was an
upfront requirement in our selection process.
Despite those representations, Peak Sales did not deliver the results that were committed
to. At this point, one ISR position remains unfulfilled, a full year after we started this
process, and we have incurred substantial time, effort, and internal cost as a result. We
also have concerns about the quality and fit of the ISR hires that were made, which has
required far more management lift than expected.
Given that outcome, we are asking Peak Sales to address the following:
1. Clarify the ISR fee structure and how fees were allocated.
2. Confirm why TEVET should bear the full fee associated with the unfulfilled ISR
portion of the engagement.
3. Refund $13,500 representing the fee paid for that unfulfilled portion of the ISR
contract.
4. Address the $64,500 in training costs associated with that ISR position and how Peak
proposes to make TEVET whole for those costs.
Separately, while invoice #1-6631 for $28,000 relates to a position that has been filled and
is not part of our dispute, we have held payment pending resolution of the ISR issue. In that
context, we respectfully ask that Peak Sales hold any associated interest or late charges
while we work through this in good faith.
Our goal is to resolve this professionally and directly. Please let us know what Peak Sales
is prepared to do to make this right.
Regards,
Tracy Solomon
Thank you,

hunter mentality and is motivated by winning

Summary of Peak Sales Recruiting Phone Call with Carly Van Gogh 8/26/25:

Here’s a detailed summary of all responses from Carly Van Gogh during the Peak Sales Meeting on August 26, 2025:

Company & Context

Carly clarified that although she is based in Vancouver, Peak is technically a Canadian company, with 90% of its business and workforce in the U.S..

She emphasized that Peak is highly specialized: sales recruiting is all they do, making them experts in differentiating between good and excellent sales candidates.

Partnership Philosophy

Carly stressed that Peak seeks long-term partnerships, not one-off placements.

She positioned Peak as the best sales recruiting firm in North America, committed to supporting client growth and evolving needs.

She noted that Peak wants to be a strategic partner rather than just a vendor, providing consistency and efficiency in hiring.

Recruitment Process

Kickoff Call (Day 1): Deep dive into company culture, values, roles, and growth opportunities.

Calibration Call (24 hours later): Presentation of sample profiles to align expectations and refine the ideal candidate profile.

Shortlist Delivery (within 10 business days of calibration): 3–5 fully vetted candidates per role, with detailed summaries.

Overall Timeline: Typically 30–45 days from kickoff to offer acceptance, though it can be as quick as 18 days depending on circumstances.

Candidate Evaluation & Success Rate

100% fill rate guarantee — Peak will fill vacancies, no matter how long it takes.

95% retention success rate — candidates placed remain past Peak’s guarantee period (120 days).

Process includes track record verification (sales metrics, CRM use, pipeline management, forecasting, deal size, leadership impact).

Uses psychometric assessments tailored to sales DNA to match candidates to consultative vs transactional sales roles.

Peak also validates candidates by cross-checking KPI claims to weed out exaggerations or inconsistencies.

Services & Capabilities

Executive Search: Able to handle senior positions such as Vice President of Sales.

Behavioral Interviewing: Can provide tailored questions, including ones informed by psychometric results.

Team Assessments: While not standard, Carly indicated Peak could explore assessing existing teams if needed.

Candidate Support: Handles interview scheduling, reference checks, soft negotiations, and provides complete candidate files (resume, assessments, references).

Competitive Advantage

Peak focuses exclusively on sales, unlike generalist recruiters.

Carly emphasized quality over speed, but noted Peak’s process is still significantly faster than many firms.

Peak prioritizes positive candidate experiences, ensuring professionalism and efficiency throughout.

She underscored her personal passion for sales and belief in Peak’s methodology, saying every recruiter at Peak shares the same commitment.

Next Steps

Once TEVET selects Peak, the proposal becomes a contract, executed by TEVET’s contracts manager (Sarah Zamudio).

Following contract execution, Peak introduces the dedicated account manager and sets up kickoff and calibration calls.

Carly assured that Peak’s team is already prepared and well-briefed on TEVET’s needs, ready to begin immediately upon confirmation.

✅ In summary:
Carly Van Gogh strongly positioned Peak as the premier partner for all TEVET’s sales recruiting needs, emphasizing process rigor, fast turnaround, retention success, and a long-term partnership model. She detailed their structured recruitment process, guarantees, and flexibility, while repeatedly reinforcing Peak’s exclusive focus on sales and passion for delivering top-quality candidates.

From: Tracy Solomon
To: Connor Fletcher
Cc: Donna Millard; Emilee Askin; Sean Murkar; Corky Clodfelter; John Vandewater
Subject: TEVET - Peak Sales Performance
Date: Friday, May 8, 2026 11:11:09 AM
Connor,
Following up on our prior conversations, TEVET is putting its concerns in writing so Peak
can review this matter internally and respond with a fair and expeditious resolution.
TEVET’s primary concern is the ISR portion of the engagement. TEVET’s secondary
concern is the recruiting process for the VP of Sales role, specifically the process delay
and resistance to TEVET’s required candidate-disclosure approach, not the individual
ultimately hired.
Peak was selected to solve a very targeted and critical business issue. TEVET did not
engage Peak for general sales recruiting. In March 2025 TEVET began the project to
identify and down-select a recruiting firm capable of sourcing ISR and sales candidates
from a specific set of industry suppliers/companies. This requirement was critical
because TEVET had already determined, through experience, that candidates from non-
target companies and OEM/manufacturer environments were not producing successful
ISR outcomes in TEVET’s high-volume, fast-paced, transaction-heavy business model.
Peak represented that it could execute that targeted company/industry-based recruiting
strategy and perform it better than the other firms TEVET evaluated. TEVET relied on that
representation in selecting Peak. “Go hard! Go fast! Bring in the BEST athlete!” was and
is TEVET’s mantra. Peak continues to fail at meeting this standard, even after our
meeting in March 2026.
TEVET also invested substantial time and effort to set Peak up for success. TEVET
provided specific examples of successful internal profiles, including target Account
Manager and ISR profiles from relevant industry companies. TEVET provided resumes,
job descriptions, Predictive Index information, target company/industry information, and
access to some of TEVET’s high-performing employees so Peak could understand
exactly what success looked like.
Despite this, Peak failed to deliver the performance it represented it could provide.
During our March 23, 2026 meeting, TEVET directly addressed Peak’s performance over
the prior 7 to 8 months (since August 2025). In that discussion, TEVET reiterated that a
specific list of target companies had been provided at the initial kickoff for AM and ISR
sourcing. The meeting summary also reflects that Peak’s prior recruiter did not utilize
that list, resulting in misaligned candidates and failed outcomes. These catastrophic
results continue.
A substantive point is the target-company/industry sourcing strategy was not incidental.
It was the core reason Peak was selected.
Peak later attempted to downplay this requirement by stating that TEVET had not

Page 2
directed Peak to recruit solely from the provided list. TEVET does not accept that
position. The issue is not whether Peak was prohibited from considering any other
candidate. The issue is that Peak was selected because it represented that it could
successfully recruit from the target supplier universe. If Peak did not intend to execute
that strategy, or believed it was impractical, Peak should have raised that before
accepting the engagement.
Peak also resisted TEVET’s instruction that candidates needed to know TEVET was the
hiring company. Given TEVET’s industry position and vertical, this was an important
requirement. TEVET specifically believed that leading with the TEVET name would attract
stronger candidates from targeted industry companies. Instead, Peak continued using a
passive approach that withheld TEVET’s name, which contributed to poor response,
process inefficiency, and delay. We were later told by at least one candidate that they
would have responded earlier had they known the opportunity was with TEVET.
TEVET received feedback that a Peak-sourced hire felt encouraged to “give it a try”
despite concerns about role fit, then resigned after approximately four weeks. That kind
of outcome is inconsistent with the level of candidate assessment, qualification, and fit
evaluation TEVET expected from a specialized sales recruiting firm.
The ISR results have been unacceptable. TEVET has experienced exactly what it hired
Peak to prevent: a cycle of hiring, training, managing performance issues, termination,
replacement, and continued instability. Yet another ISR was let go this week. This
confirms that the issue is not an isolated candidate mismatch. It reflects a repeated
breakdown in Peak’s sourcing, screening, assessment, and candidate-fit process.
A continued replacement process is not an adequate remedy. The replacement process
itself has become part of the damage. TEVET cannot continue absorbing the cost and
disruption of failed placements produced by the same process that created the
problem.
By the March 23, 2026 meeting, TEVET had already spent over $50,000 and raised the
possibility of requesting a refund due to lost time, resources, and delays in critical hiring.
Since then, the harm has continued. TEVET has incurred additional internal cost,
management burden, onboarding effort, lost productivity, operational disruption, and
legal and employee-relations exposure. To date, TEVET has spent over $75,000. TEVET
hired and onboarded a Manager of Recruitment and Onboarding to independently build
the candidate pipeline that Peak failed to produce.
TEVET’s position is that Peak’s performance has been egregious and damaging. Peak
accepted a clearly defined recruiting assignment, represented that it had the capability
to perform it, failed to execute the required sourcing strategy, resisted TEVET’s
candidate-disclosure requirement, produced substandard candidates over an extended
period, and transferred the financial, operational, managerial, and legal risk back onto
TEVET. This is unacceptable and cannot be tolerated.

Page 3
TEVET is requesting the following:
1. Discontinue replacement activity for ISR candidates that are not working out and
refund $13,500 of the ISR contract.
2. Address TEVET’s estimated $64,500 in conservative training, onboarding,
management, and related costs associated with the failed ISR hiring cycle.
3. Hold any interest or late charges associated with invoice #1-6631 for $28,000
while the parties work through Peak’s performance issues in good faith.
TEVET expects this matter to be addressed expeditiously. Please provide Peak’s written
response no later than end of day Friday, May 15, 2026.
TEVET remains willing to resolve this professionally and directly, but we are not willing to
extend the same failed process or absorb the continuing cost of Peak’s
nonperformance. TEVET reserves all rights while we evaluate the full impact of Peak’s
performance failure.
Regards,
Tracy Solomon – CEO
